Title: Fumarylacetoacetate hydrolase targeted by a Fusarium graminearum effector positively regulates wheat FHB resistance

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Secreted effector protein with N-terminal signal peptide; no other reported domains
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted from fungus and translocated into host cell cytoplasm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced during wheat infection, especially at 24 and 48 hpi during cushion-mediated penetration stage; negligible at 0 hpi
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Higher amino acid levels are associated with increased resistance to Fusarium graminearum; some amino acids also reduce DON accumulation and inhibit fungal growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Fusarium graminearum virulence by reducing host TaFAH abundance, lowering host amino acid accumulation, and suppressing wheat FHB resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout and complementation in F. graminearum; wheat head infection assay; fungal biomass and DON quantification; secretion/translocation assay; ROS and callose assays; Y2H; IP-MS; pull-down; LCI; Co-IP; BiFC; cell-free degradation assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wheat infection; flg22 or chitin elicitation in Nicotiana benthamiana; MG132 proteasome inhibitor
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FgEC10 is a secreted and translocated Fusarium effector required for full virulence on wheat heads. 2. FgEC10 suppresses basal immunity, including ROS burst, callose deposition, and expression of TaPR5 and TaMAPK3. 3. FgEC10 directly binds wheat TaFAH and enhances its degradation through the 26S proteasome by promoting TaFAH interaction with TaPSMD12. 4. Loss of FgEC10 reduces disease index, fungal biomass, and DON content in infected wheat heads.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Deletion of FgEC10 did not affect colony morphology, growth, infection cushion formation, or DON biosynthesis in liquid medium; reduced DON in spikes was attributed to reduced infection spread rather than altered fungal DON biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathogen effector probe for identifying host resistance targets; disrupting FgEC10 function or its interaction with TaFAH could be useful for engineering FHB resistance
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics used for candidate effector selection and host RNA-seq; NCBI SRA PRJNA1070554. IP-MS used to identify candidate interacting proteins. Metabolomics datasets: MassIVE MSV000097681; MSV000097684

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly up-regulated after Fusarium graminearum infection of wheat heads, then returned to normal levels
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced in infected wheat heads; overexpressed in transgenic wheat lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: His, Lys, and Ser enhance wheat resistance to Fusarium graminearum; Lys additionally inhibits fungal growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression and RNAi in wheat; disease assays; fungal biomass and DON measurement; RNA-seq; untargeted and targeted metabolomics; enzyme activity assay via acetoacetate detection; protein interaction and degradation assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; flowering stage metabolomics; MG132 treatment in protein degradation and acetoacetate assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaFAH positively regulates wheat resistance to Fusarium graminearum. 2. Overexpression decreases disease index, fungal biomass, and DON content, whereas RNAi increases susceptibility. 3. TaFAH overexpression causes earlier heading and induces defense- and flowering-related gene expression. 4. TaFAH overexpression increases accumulation of several amino acids associated with enhanced resistance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Wheat contains three highly similar homeoalleles/paralogs: TaFAH, TaFAH-B, and TaFAH-D, suggesting functional redundancy; FgEC10 does not alter TaFAH catalytic activity directly but promotes its degradation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant penalties reported for plant height, spike length, grain number per spike, or thousand-grain weight; heading is advanced by about 10 days
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable resistance gene resource for improving FHB resistance and earlier heading; promoter editing or expression enhancement suggested for breeding
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA1070554; metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000097681; MSV000097684
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter for overexpression in wheat

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 26S proteasome regulatory particle non-ATPase subunit 12 (PSMD12/RPN5 homolog)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Through degrading TaFAH, indirectly affects amino acid accumulation associated with FHB resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS implication; pull-down; LCI; Co-IP; co-localization; in vitro degradation assay; yeast tri-hybrid
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MG132-sensitive 26S proteasome pathway during wheat infection/protein degradation assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaPSMD12 directly interacts with TaFAH both in vitro and in vivo. 2. TaPSMD12 accelerates TaFAH degradation in a 26S proteasome-dependent manner. 3. FgEC10 enhances the interaction between TaFAH and TaPSMD12, thereby promoting TaFAH degradation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FgEC10 and TaPSMD12 did not promote TaFAH ubiquitination, indicating ubiquitin-independent facilitation of proteasomal degradation in this context
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS; RNA-seq and metabolomics are study-level datasets: PRJNA1070554; MSV000097681; MSV000097684

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyrosine degradation linked to TCA cycle and amino acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step of tyrosine catabolism feeding carbon into the TCA cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolytically cleaves fumarylacetoacetate into fumarate and acetoacetate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor/carbon flux into TCA cycle that supports amino acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Elevated amino acids, especially His, Lys, and Ser, enhance wheat resistance to Fusarium graminearum; Lys also inhibits fungal growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of several amino acids and enhances wheat FHB resistance while accelerating heading
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression and RNAi in wheat; acetoacetate product assay in tobacco; RNA-seq; untargeted and targeted metabolomics; infection phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced after Fusarium graminearum infection, then declines to basal levels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced in infected heads; highly expressed in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; flowering-stage metabolomics; MG132 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaFAH encodes an active fumarylacetoacetate hydrolase producing acetoacetate. 2. Its overexpression increases amino acid accumulation and resistance to FHB, while RNAi causes susceptibility. 3. FgEC10 targets TaFAH for proteasomal degradation through TaPSMD12. 4. TaFAH overexpression also promotes earlier heading.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FgEC10 does not alter intrinsic enzymatic activity of TaFAH when co-expressed in tobacco; the primary effect is on protein stability
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Earlier heading (~10 days); no major negative agronomic penalties reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for resistance breeding or metabolic engineering; promoter editing/expression enhancement proposed by authors
